title: helenium laciniatum
intro: Helenium laciniatum, also known as the sneezeweed, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its yellow daisy-like flowers and divided leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Helenium laciniatum can be prepared as an herbal remedy in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
